prediction.summary: An attacker (10.20.8.77) successfully uploaded a PHP web shell (`shell.php`) to the `/uploads/` directory of the web server. Following the upload, the attacker used the web shell to execute commands such as `whoami`, `cat /etc/passwd`, and attempted to download a second-stage payload. Multiple security systems (WAF, IDS, ModSecurity, SIEM) detected and alerted on this malicious activity, confirming the presence and active use of a web shell.

timeline.event: WAF alerted on suspicious PHP upload to webroot `/uploads/shell.php` from 10.20.8.77.
Attacker executed `whoami` command via the web shell.
Attacker attempted to read `/etc/passwd` via the web shell.
Attacker attempted to download `stage2` payload from `http://evil.tld/stage2`.
IDS detected web shell activity (repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter).
SIEM correlated events to MITRE T1505.003 (Web Shell).
ModSecurity blocked a SQL injection probe from 10.20.8.77 via the web shell.
Critical SIEM alert for web shell activity on webserver01.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised web server (webserver01) to prevent further compromise and lateral movement.
Perform a forensic analysis of the web server to identify the full extent of compromise, including persistence mechanisms, other uploaded files, and any data exfiltration.
Block the attacker's IP address (10.20.8.77) at the perimeter firewall and other security controls.
Review and enhance file upload validation mechanisms to prevent similar web shell uploads in the future.
Investigate `http://evil.tld/stage2` for additional threat intelligence and indicators of compromise.
